entries.title: Separatist Crisis Defines the Pre-War Republic

content.fact: By Attack of the Clones, the Republic faces a Separatist crisis but has not yet openly begun the Clone Wars.
content.injection: During early Episode II, frame the Republic as politically strained by secession, assassination fears, and military debate, not yet as a wartime Empire.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Separatist Crisis Intensifies.
Respect story-position bounds from Separatist Crisis Intensifies to Separatist Crisis Intensifies.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.
content.notes: Generated development draft; verify during Saga review.

content.fact: Padme is a senator and assassination target during the Separatist crisis, not Naboo’s reigning queen.
content.injection: In Episode II context, write Padme as Senator Amidala with political agency and personal danger, not as the current Queen of Naboo.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Separatist Crisis Intensifies.
Respect story-position bounds from Separatist Crisis Intensifies to Separatist Crisis Intensifies.

content.fact: Obi-Wan traces the assassination plot to Kamino and discovers the Republic’s clone army.
content.injection: In Clone Army Discovered on Kamino, once Obi-Wan reaches Kamino, the clone army becomes discoverable to Jedi leadership but is not yet broadly understood by the galaxy.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Clone Army Discovered on Kamino.
Respect story-position bounds from Clone Army Discovered on Kamino to Clone Army Discovered on Kamino.

content.fact: The clone army’s origin is suspicious and politically convenient, even though the Republic accepts it in crisis.
content.injection: Do not portray the clone army as a cleanly explained Republic project; its origin should feel opaque, alarming, and too useful.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Clone Army Discovered on Kamino.
Respect story-position bounds from Clone Army Discovered on Kamino to Clone Army Discovered on Kamino.

entries.title: Jango Is Clone Template and Boba’s Father

content.fact: Jango Fett is the genetic template for the clone army and raises Boba Fett as his unaltered son.
content.injection: In Episode II context, connect Jango to the clone army and Boba’s origin; after Geonosis, Boba’s hostility toward Jedi has a personal basis.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Clone Army Discovered on Kamino.
Respect story-position bounds from Clone Army Discovered on Kamino to Clone Army Discovered on Kamino.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.
content.notes: Generated development draft; verify during Saga review.

context.label: Episode II: Attack of the Clones

content.fact: Anakin’s protection assignment places him in close contact with Padme and accelerates their forbidden attachment.
content.injection: During Episode II, Anakin and Padme’s relationship is intimate and risky, but not yet publicly acknowledged or legitimate under Jedi rules.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Episode II: Attack of the Clones.

context.label: Episode II: Attack of the Clones

content.fact: Anakin shows intense attachment, impatience, and resentment while still functioning as Obi-Wan’s Padawan.
content.injection: Write Episode II Anakin as powerful and emotionally volatile, but still a Padawan under Obi-Wan’s authority.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Episode II: Attack of the Clones.

entries.title: Tusken Massacre Is Private Dark Turn

context.label: Episode II: Attack of the Clones

content.fact: Anakin massacres Tusken Raiders after Shmi’s death, but this is not public Jedi knowledge by default.
content.injection: Treat the Tusken massacre as a private dark-side rupture known only to characters the scene establishes, especially Anakin and Padme.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Episode II: Attack of the Clones.
Respect story-position bounds from Episode II: Attack of the Clones to Anakin and Padme Secretly Marry.

context.label: Episode II: Attack of the Clones

content.fact: Count Dooku is publicly the Separatist leader while privately Darth Tyranus, a Sith Lord.
content.injection: During Episode II, ordinary characters may know Dooku as Separatist leader but should not automatically know his Sith role or Sidious connection.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Episode II: Attack of the Clones.

content.fact: The Geonosis crisis exposes Separatist military strength and causes major Jedi casualties before the clones intervene.
content.injection: At Geonosis, portray the Jedi as brave but outmatched by the scale of industrial warfare until clone forces arrive.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Geonosis Arena Crisis.
Respect story-position bounds from Geonosis Arena Crisis to Geonosis Arena Crisis.

content.fact: The Battle of Geonosis turns the Separatist crisis into the Clone Wars.
content.injection: After Geonosis, the Republic is at war and Jedi leadership shifts toward military command roles. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Clone Wars Begin.

context.label: After Attack of the Clones, before Revenge of the Sith

content.fact: After Geonosis, Jedi increasingly operate as generals and commanders in the Republic war effort.
content.injection: In post-AOTC Clone Wars context, Jedi can command clone forces, but this role is a wartime change, not their normal prewar function.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: After Attack of the Clones, before Revenge of the Sith.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.

entries.title: Anakin Loses His Arm to Dooku

content.fact: Anakin loses an arm in the duel with Dooku and receives a mechanical replacement.
content.injection: After Attack of the Clones, Anakin has a cybernetic arm; before that injury, do not describe him with the later prosthetic.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Clone Wars Begin.
Respect story-position bounds from Clone Wars Begin to Clone Wars Begin.

entries.title: Palpatine Not Exposed Before Revenge of the Sith

context.label: After Attack of the Clones, before Revenge of the Sith

content.fact: Before Revenge of the Sith, Palpatine’s identity as Sidious is not public and not known to the Jedi Order.
content.injection: In Clone Wars pre-ROTS context, do not have Jedi characters casually know Palpatine is Darth Sidious unless the story introduces that divergence.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: After Attack of the Clones, before Revenge of the Sith.

entries.title: Anakin Not Darth Vader Before His Fall

context.label: After Attack of the Clones, before Revenge of the Sith

content.fact: Before his fall in Revenge of the Sith, Anakin is not publicly or functionally Darth Vader.
content.injection: Do not call pre-fall Anakin Darth Vader in-character unless using prophecy, private Sith planning, or explicit future knowledge.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: After Attack of the Clones, before Revenge of the Sith.
